authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Bullards Bar Reservoir not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Bullards Bar Reservoir not listed)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW
sources.quotedText: R2 Yuba Bullards Bar Reservoir Eagle Lake Trout Sub-catchable Put and Grow January & December; R2 Yuba Bullards Bar Reservoir Kokanee Salmon Fingerling Put and Grow April-June

sources.title: Wikidata Q7006203 — New Bullards Bar Reservoir, GNIS Feature ID 267058 (coordinate claim P625); USGS GNIS ArcGIS mirror (carto.nationalmap.gov MapServer/7, layer for lakes/reservoirs/ponds) has no record under gaz_id 267058 or any "Bullards Bar"-named feature in Yuba/Sierra Co., CA, so this GNIS-ID-linked structured-data record is used per the dataset's no-clean-GNIS-match fallback. Corroborated by the Wikipedia infobox for "New Bullards Bar Reservoir" (39°25′45″N 121°07′48″W / 39.42903°N 121.13010°W) — distinct from the separate "New Bullards Bar Dam" article, whose infobox coordinate (39.39333°N 121.14306°W) is for the dam structure itself, ~1.1km south of the reservoir point used here.

parameters.note: Bullards Bar Reservoir is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation in §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit. This general limit applies to Eagle Lake Trout and other trout at Bullards Bar Reservoir; Kokanee salmon (a landlocked salmon) instead carries the special CCR §5.41(e)(1) bag/possession limit for New Bullards Bar Reservoir (see the separate Kokanee salmon bag regulation below).

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit (Eagle Lake Trout and other trout; Kokanee salmon has its own §5.41(e)(1) limit, see below)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: CCR T14 §5.41(e)(1) Landlocked Salmon special regulation naming New Bullards Bar Reservoir (Yuba Co.): daily bag limit of ten and possession limit of twenty for Kokanee salmon (landlocked salmon), superseding the general 5/10 statewide trout limit for this species at this water.

regulations.humanSummary: Kokanee salmon (landlocked salmon) special bag limit at New Bullards Bar Reservoir: 10 daily / 20 possession, per CCR §5.41(e)(1) — supersedes the general 5/10 trout limit for this species
regulations.verbatimText: New Bullards Bar Reservoir (Yuba Co.) is named under CCR T14 §5.41(e)(1)'s Landlocked Salmon exception with special limits of a daily bag of ten and a possession limit of twenty.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

stockingSchedule.note: CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Bullards Bar Reservoir (Region 2, Yuba Co.) for sub-catchable-size Eagle Lake Trout under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month of 'January & December'. Encoded here as a December-through-January winter window; this is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.

CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Bullards Bar Reservoir (Region 2, Yuba Co.) for fingerling-size Kokanee Salmon under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month range of April-June. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.
